meaning: n.(墙壁等的)凹进处,[解]隐窝vt.使凹进vi.休假,休息
examples: She decides to visit the school library during recess.
It seemed to him that the noon recess would never come.
At recess Tom continued his flirtation with Amy with jubilant self-satisfaction.
Plenty there for MPs - and hacks - to think about as we disappear for the parliamentary recess.
His goal: to get a bill through each house of Congress by the August recess.
